assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.
Implement a module of a 16-bit full adder in combinational logic.
y[15:0]: 16-bit output representing the sum of A and B.
In the adder_16bit module, you need to design a small bit-width adder(8-bit adder), which will be instantiated multiple times.
Task description was provided and only used as a low-risk planning hint.
Spec text was provided and only used where it did not contradict the contract.

expected_properties: Observable outputs respond to legal input changes without assuming internal state.
Checks focus on input/output consistency rather than cycle-accurate timing.
Co equals the carry-out of the 16‑bit addition
cases.goal: Validate combinational sum functionality across random legal inputs
notes: Case intent is conservative when the contract is weak or timing is unresolved.
Ensures basic functional correctness without assuming timing
preconditions: Use only ports and observable signals present in the contract.
stimulus_intent: Drive a representative legal input pattern across known inputs: ['a', 'b', 'Cin']
Apply random legal values to a, b, and Cin
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Outputs are expected to settle after input stabilization; sample after a short propagation delay

expected_properties: Boundary-value stimulation does not rely on hidden state or undocumented latency.
When width expressions are symbolic, only conservative min/max-style exploration is planned.
y correctly reflects overflow behavior at maximum operand values
Co correctly indicates overflow for boundary conditions
cases.goal: Exercise boundary‑value patterns (all‑zeros, all‑ones, mixed extremes) on a, b, and Cin
notes: Edge coverage remains value-oriented and avoids fixed-latency assumptions.
Focuses on extreme values to expose width‑related edge cases
preconditions: At least one input or symbolic-width input is available.
stimulus_intent: Use zero-like, one-like, and boundary patterns on ['a', 'b']
Apply zero‑like, one‑like, and mixed boundary patterns to a, b, and Cin
timing_assumptions: Do not infer internal state or undocumented storage.
Observe outputs after inputs have stabilized; no fixed‑cycle latency assumed

expected_properties: Outputs become X when any input is X
No deterministic value is produced when inputs contain X/Z
cases.goal: Verify that unknown/illegal input values propagate to outputs as per combinational logic
notes: Testing X/Z propagation on a, b, and Cin
stimulus_intent: Drive X to each input individually and observe output behavior
timing_assumptions: Observe outputs after inputs have stabilized
